summary: This is a technical white paper describing Unichain, an optimistic rollup blockchain technology built on the OP Stack for decentralized finance (DeFi) applications. The document focuses on blockchain infrastructure, block building mechanisms, MEV (maximal extractable value) optimization, and validator networks - all related to cryptocurrency/blockchain technology rather than biotechnology or life sciences.

content_blocks.title: Abstract and Introduction
content_blocks.content: Unichain is an optimistic rollup designed for efficient cryptocurrency markets with fast state updates (200-250ms block times via Flashblocks), MEV internalization framework, and economic finality system. Built on Ethereum's OP Stack as part of the Superchain network. Addresses DeFi challenges including execution quality, user experience, and liquidity fragmentation.

content_blocks.title: Prior Work and Current Challenges
content_blocks.content: Reviews Ethereum scaling challenges, rollup technologies from OP Labs and Offchain Labs, MEV extraction issues, and automated market maker (AMM) limitations. References prior work on gas fees, private mempools, TEEs, and sandwich attacks in DeFi applications.

content_blocks.title: Verifiable Block Building
content_blocks.content: Describes sequencer-builder separation using TEE (Trusted Execution Environment) on Intel TDX hardware. Features include: Flashblocks (sub-second block pre-confirmations), priority ordering enforcement, trustless revert protection for failed transactions. Open source builder codebase available on GitHub.

content_blocks.title: Unichain Validation Network
content_blocks.content: Decentralized network of node operators using UNI token staking on Ethereum mainnet to validate blocks and provide faster economic finality. Addresses block equivocation risk and invalid block risk to enable faster cross-chain settlement.

content_blocks.title: Future Work and Conclusion
content_blocks.content: Potential extensions include encrypted mempool, scheduled transactions, TEE coprocessor, credible neutrality monitoring. Aims to address liquidity fragmentation in Ethereum's rollup ecosystem for DeFi applications.

key_findings: Blockchain infrastructure technology for cryptocurrency DeFi applications
Not related to biotechnology, therapeutics, diagnostics, or life sciences research
No experimental data, clinical evidence, or biological research presented
Technology focuses on distributed ledger systems, not TRL-assessable biotech projects
